recommendations: stabilize dependency detection by reading `pyproject.toml` from the extracted tree (it exists but was flagged missing)
add a preflight validation step that checks test-status payloads are non-empty and fails fast with actionable diagnostics
trim MCP endpoint surface by exposing only high-value stable APIs first (e.g.
`read_raw_*`
`Epochs`
`read_evokeds`
core inverse/beamformer calls) and gate plotting/UI-heavy methods behind optional feature flags
add contract tests for each advertised endpoint (import
call
type/shape assertions
expected exceptions) plus a small golden-dataset smoke suite
introduce CI matrix for minimal vs full optional dependencies (headless + viz extras) to catch import/runtime drift early
enforce structured error mapping in the adapter (MNE errors → consistent MCP error codes/messages) with correlation IDs for debugging
improve performance by lazy-importing heavy modules (`viz`
`pyvista`
Qt backends) and measuring cold-start latency in CI with thresholds
add timeout/retry/circuit-breaker controls around long-running operations (`Raw.filter`
inverse/beamformer computations) and document defaults
implement resource guards for memory/CPU on large FIF processing (chunking/streaming guidance
max duration/sample caps)
harden input validation with pydantic schemas per endpoint (file path existence
channel picks
frequency bounds
event IDs)
add security checks for untrusted file handling (path traversal protection
allowed extensions
sandboxed temp dirs)
create a compatibility policy/version pinning strategy for MNE + optional ecosystem packages with automated weekly update PRs
add observability (basic metrics: request count
latency
failure rate by endpoint) and a troubleshooting section in `README_MCP.md`
reduce repo-analysis noise by excluding massive nonessential metadata folders (e.g.
`doc/sphinxext/prs`) from plugin-generation context
add end-to-end CLI tests for `start_mcp.py` startup/shutdown and health checks
include example client snippets and minimal reproducible workflows in README for Raw→Epochs→Evoked pipeline
add regression tests specifically for adapter mode `import` fallback behavior and clear downgrade path to CLI mode
define an endpoint deprecation/versioning scheme to prevent breaking downstream MCP clients

success_factors: Workflow completed all planned nodes and reached finalize with overall status=success.
Zip fallback recovered repository content after git clone network failure (analysis reports ~8,380 files).
Import-mode adapter generation succeeded and produced runnable MCP scaffold files.
MCP plugin health check passed (healthy over stdio), indicating baseline service boot viability.
failure_reasons: Primary clone failed due to network timeout to github.com:443 (curl 28), causing degraded acquisition path.
Telemetry/metrics integrity issues: total_files_processed=0 and zeroed performance metrics despite large analyzed tree.
Dependency/entry-point detection produced false negatives (pyproject/environment.yml exist but flagged missing).
Validation quality gap: original project test status marked failed with empty payload/details.

node_performance.download_time: ~75s+ likely spent on failed clone retry/timeout before fallback; dominant delay source.
node_performance.analysis_time: Completed with large corpus via fallback; likely substantial but not directly instrumented per-node.
node_performance.generation_time: Fast scaffold generation succeeded, but generated LOC/size/tool_count were recorded as 0 (instrumentation defect).
node_performance.test_time: MCP health check succeeded quickly; original-project tests not meaningfully executed/validated.

resource_usage.memory_efficiency: Unknown due to missing metrics; likely moderate pressure when indexing very large docs/metadata tree.
resource_usage.cpu_efficiency: Unknown/zero-reported; analysis likely CPU-bound but telemetry is not trustworthy.
resource_usage.disk_usage: Potentially high due to extracted zip + generated outputs; no quantitative tracking available.
